rag_rules: Preserve the AADL architecture by inserting AGREE annex blocks only in valid component type or implementation scopes
Place component type assumptions and guarantees in the component type annex
Place implementation-level bindings in the component implementation annex
Do not use subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless represented through valid variables or outputs
generic_rules: Insert AGREE annex blocks in the appropriate scope to maintain architectural integrity
Preserve the existing AADL structure during fusion
Use only visible identifiers that are explicitly defined in the AADL model
Do not introduce new identifiers or assumptions without clear requirements
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are written inside `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks
Use concrete bounds, named constants, or visible relations for assumptions and guarantees
Relate outputs to inputs in component type contracts using visible identifiers
anti_patterns: Do not use implementation-only subcomponent paths in component type annexes
Do not place assumptions or guarantees in invalid scopes
Do not introduce new identifiers or assumptions without clear requirements
Do not use subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless represented through valid variables or outputs
irrelevant_topics: AGREE, annex, assume, guarantee, assign, validator, or repair
